zh.Lévis-Saint-Nom, Yvelines, Île-de-France: 法國i萊-德-福朗斯易維林斯列維-賽吉諾姆
zh.Roche-en-Régnier, Haute-Loire, Auvergne-: 法國奧維-o-阿爾培斯豪-洛易洛--雷聶
zh.lifeSummary: 當Jourdaine De La Roche En Regnier於1130年出生在法國奧維-o-阿爾培斯豪-洛易洛--雷聶時，她的父親Guigues Ier DE LA ROCHE EN REGNIER15歲，母親Pontia ?10歲。她和Bertrand De Levis至少有1個兒子。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: La
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Roche
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Aidan, Declan, Eamonn, Fergus, Fionnuala, Kevin Patrick, Liam.
Irish (Munster) and English (of Norman origin): variant of   Roach  . Compare   Roch  .

French: topographic name for someone who lived by a rocky outcrop or crag, from Old French roche ‘rock’. In North America, this surname is also a shortened form of the variant Laroche 2. Compare Deroche .

variants.name: Laroche
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Fernand, Normand, Pierre, Jacques, Laurent, Adrien, Cecile, Germain, Marcel.
French:
habitational name from any of numerous places in various parts of France named Laroche or La Roche, from Old French roche ‘rock’, with feminine definite article la.

variants.name: Larocque
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Fernand, Henri, Marcel, Normand, Valmore, Yves, Achille, Amie, Donat, Emile.

French: habitational name from La Ro(c)que, the name of several places in various parts of France, from a regional variant of roche ‘rock’ (see Roche , compare Laroche ), with feminine definite article la. Compare Laroque .

Altered form of southern French Roquebrune or of its variant Rocquebrune: habitational name from Ro(c)quebrune, the name of several places in the southern part of France. The surname Roquebrune is rare in France, while the variant Rocquebrune is extinct there. Compare Larock , Lerocque , Rockburn , and Rocque 1.

variants.name: Rochet
meanings: French: diminutive of   Roche  , a topographic name meaning ‘rock’, or a habitational name from (Le) Rochet, the name of several places in various parts of France.
Catalan: of French origin (see above). This surname is also found in the Dominican Republic and Puerto Rico.

variants.name: Rochat
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Monique, Philippe, Pierre.
French: topographic name from a diminutive of Old French roche ‘rock’ (see Roche 2), or a habitational name from Le Rochat, the name of several places in the eastern part of France.

variants.name: Roach
meanings: English:

of Norman origin, from Old French and Middle English roche ‘rock, cliff, promontory’, sometimes a translation of Stone . The surname may have been topographic, denoting someone who lived on or by a prominent rock or rocky place, or a habitational name for someone who lived or came from a place so named, such as one of those called Roque or Roche(s) in Normandy and neighboring regions, or Roche in Cornwall, Roach Farm in Clyst Hydon (Devon), or Roch in Pembrokeshire (see Roch ).

in Lancashire perhaps referring to the River Roch, which runs through Rochdale; the river name is probably a back-formation from Recedham, an early name for Rochdale recorded in 1086, derived from Old English reced ‘building, house, hall’ + hām ‘village, homestead’. Alternatively, the Lancashire name could be a shortened form of the now rare or extinct surname Rochdale alias Rachedale, Rachdall, a habitational name from Rochdale (Lancashire). From the 18th century onward the name is difficult to distinguish from Roch , borne by Irish migrants to South Lancashire, especially Liverpool.

variants.name: Roquet
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alain, Camille.
French: topographic name from a diminutive of roche ‘rock’ (see Roche and Roque ), or a habitational name from (Le) Roquet, the name of several places in various parts of France.

variants.name: Rochez
meanings: Walloon and French: variant of   Roche   2 or   Rocher  .

nameMeanings.name: En
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 任, based on its Mandarin pronunciation, see   Ren   1.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 全, based on its pronunciation in Shanghai dialect, see   Quan   2.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 曾, see   Zeng  .

variants.name: Ren
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Yong, Hong, Hui, Chuan, Gang, Guang, Jian, Liang, Lihua, Ning, Qing, Chang, Min, Shen, Yiming.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 任, a term for women's rank of peerage in ancient Chinese: (i) it is said that the surname can be traced back to one of the 25 sons of the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). (ii) from Ren (任), the name of an ancient state (located in Renxian in Hebei province) granted to Yu Yang, said to be a son or grandson of the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). His descendants adopted the name of the state as their surname. (iii) from Ren (任), the name of a state (located in Shandong province) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ).
English: variant of   Renn   2.

nameMeanings.name: Regnier
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Lucien, Micheline, Pierre, Andre, Celestine, Felicie, Guilene, Marcel, Marcelle, Patrice, Romain.
French (also Régnier): from the personal name Regnier, Régnier, from ancient Germanic Raginhari, composed of the elements ragin ‘counsel’ and hari ‘army’. Compare Rainier , Reinier , and Renier .

History: Pierre Régnier from La Rochelle in Charente-Maritime, France, married Marie-Madeleine Charles in Longueuil, QC, in 1718. — This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of South Carolina.

variants.name: Rainier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Patrice.
Altered form of French   Regnier  , a cognate of 2 below. Compare   Rainear  ,   Rainer  ,   Rinear  ,   Rineer  , and   Rinier  .

In some cases possibly also French: from the personal name Rainier, from ancient Germanic Raginhari, composed of the elements ragin ‘counsel’ and hari ‘army’. This surname, which is a variant of Reinier , is very rare in France.

variants.name: Regner
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Alois, Kurt, Erwin, Franz, Otto.
South German (Bavaria): habitational name for someone from Regen (a place on the Regen river, for which it is named).
German: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements ragin ‘counsel’ + hari, heri ‘army’. This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine).

variants.name: Renier
meanings: French (also Rénier): variant of Regnier and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this (compare Rainier ).

variants.name: Reinier
meanings: French: from the personal name Reinier, from ancient Germanic Raginhari, composed of the elements ragin ‘counsel’ and hari ‘army’.
Altered form of French   Regnier  , a cognate of 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: Jourdaine
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: (French) See Jordan .

en.lifeSummary: When Jourdaine De La Roche En Regnier was born in 1130, in Roche-en-Régnier, Haute-Loire, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, France, her father, Guigues Ier DE LA ROCHE EN REGNIER, was 15 and her mother, Pontia ?, was 10. She had at least 1 son with Bertrand De Levis.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jourdaine De La Roche En Regnier est née en 1130, à Roche-en-Régnier, Haute-Loire, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, France, son père, Guigues Ier DE LA ROCHE EN REGNIER, avait 15 ans et sa mère, Pontia ?, avait 10 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec Bertrand De Levis.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jourdaine De La Roche En Regnier 1130 in Roche-en-Régnier, Haute-Loire, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Guigues Ier DE LA ROCHE EN REGNIER 15 und ihre Mutter Pontia ? 10. Sie hatte mit Bertrand De Levis mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jourdaine De La Roche En Regnier è nata il nel 1130 nel Roche-en-Régnier, Haute-Loire, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, Francia, sua padre, Guigues Ier DE LA ROCHE EN REGNIER, aveva 15 anni e sua madre, Pontia ?, aveva 10 anni. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio con Bertrand De Levis.

ja.lifeSummary: Jourdaine De La Roche En Regnierが1130年にフランスアウバーグネ-ルhoネ-アルペスハウテ-ロイアーロチェ-エン-ルeグニールで生まれた時，父Guigues Ier DE LA ROCHE EN REGNIERは15歳，母Pontia ?は10歳であった。彼女はBertrand De Levisとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Jourdaine De La Roche En Regnier(이)가 1130년 프랑스 오버그니-ho니-알프스 하테-로이르 록헤--e그니어에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Guigues Ier DE LA ROCHE EN REGNIER(은)는 15세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Pontia ?(은)는 10세였다. 그녀는 Bertrand De Levis(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jourdaine De La Roche En Regnier nasceu em 1130, em Roche-en-Régnier, Haute-Loire, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, França, seu pai, Guigues Ier DE LA ROCHE EN REGNIER, tinha 15 anos e sua mãe, Pontia ?, tinha 10 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho com Bertrand De Levis.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jourdaine De La Roche En Regnier родилась в 1130 г. в местности роше-ен-рeгниир, хауте-лоир, аувиргне-рoне-алпесь, Франция, её отцу по имени Guigues Ier DE LA ROCHE EN REGNIER было 15 лет, а матери, которую звали Pontia ?, 10. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын от Bertrand De Levis.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jourdaine De La Roche En Regnier nació en 1130, en Roche-en-Régnier, Haute-Loire, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, Francia, su papá, Guigues Ier DE LA ROCHE EN REGNIER, tenía 15 años y su mamá, Pontia ?, tenía 10 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Bertrand De Levis.
